false-positives.yml:
# False Positives – Domain Memory
# Agents: consult this before flagging a secret or security issue.
# If a finding matches an entry here, note it as a known false positive.

false_positives:
  secrets:
    - pattern: "MY_GEMINI_API_KEY"
      reason: >
        Placeholder value in .env.example. Not a real API key.
        The literal string "MY_GEMINI_API_KEY" is intentional documentation.
    - pattern: "MY_APP_URL"
      reason: >
        Placeholder value in .env.example. Not a real URL or secret.

  security_findings:
    - finding: "process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY used in constructor"
      file: src/services/aiService.ts
      reason: >
        The AIService constructor reads GEMINI_API_KEY from process.env.
        This is correct server-side usage. The key is never sent to the browser.
        Flag only if this appears in a client-side bundle or .tsx file.

    - finding: "hardcoded model name string"
      reason: >
        Gemini model name strings like "gemini-3-flash-preview" are not secrets.
        They are public API identifiers.

    - finding: "base64 string in test fixtures"
      reason: >
        Base64 encoded test data or image fixtures are not secrets.
        Only flag base64 strings that decode to key-like patterns.

  review_noise:
    - Missing return type on React event handlers is acceptable in this codebase.
    - any[] for Gemini history array is an acceptable type until Gemini SDK exports history types.
    - ProGuard warnings about missing classes from third-party AAR files are expected.

# Update this file when Claude or Gemini agents flag recurring false positives.
# Format: add an entry under the appropriate section with pattern, file (optional), and reason.
last_updated: "2026-04-05"
gemini-integration.yml:
# Gemini Integration – Domain Memory
# Agents: read this before reviewing anything in src/services/ or src/views/AIView.tsx

gemini_integration:
  entry_point: src/services/aiService.ts
  sdk: "@google/genai"
  api_key_env: GEMINI_API_KEY
  api_key_pattern: "AIza[0-9A-Za-z\\-_]{35}"

  models_in_use:
    - gemini-3-flash-preview       # fast chat
    - gemini-3.1-pro-preview       # high-thinking chat, image analysis
    - gemini-3-pro-image-preview   # image generation
    - veo-3.1-fast-generate-preview  # video generation
    - gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts  # text-to-speech

  ai_settings_object:
    location: src/services/aiService.ts
    key_fields:
      enabled: bool            # master switch – must be checked before any API call
      permissions.chat: bool
      permissions.imageGen: bool
      permissions.mapsGrounding: bool
      permissions.imageAnalysis: bool
      permissions.audioSpeech: bool
      permissions.highThinking: bool

  security_rules:
    - GEMINI_API_KEY must NEVER appear hardcoded in source. Only via process.env.
    - The .env.example placeholder value is "MY_GEMINI_API_KEY" – any real key pattern is a leak.
    - All AI methods must check settings.enabled AND the specific permission before proceeding.
    - Prompt strings that include user-supplied input must be inspected for injection vectors.
    - No API calls from client-side browser context – all Gemini calls must go through the server.

  prompt_injection_risk_areas:
    - aiService.chat() – message param is user-supplied
    - aiService.generateImage() – prompt param is user-supplied
    - aiService.generateVideo() – prompt param is user-supplied
    - aiService.analyzeImage() – prompt param is user-supplied

  known_patterns:
    - history array in chat() is passed directly to Gemini; validate role field values.
    - Video generation uses a polling loop; ensure timeout to prevent infinite wait.
    - TTS returns raw base64 audio data; ensure it is handled client-side only.

  env_file_rules:
    tracked_example: .env.example
    blocked_files:
      - .env
      - .env.local
      - .env.production
    gitignore_pattern: ".env*"
    gitignore_exception: "!.env.example"
android-conventions.yml:
# Android Conventions – Domain Memory
# Agents: read this before reviewing anything in app/

android_project:
  structure:
    app_src: app/src/main/java/com/
    manifest: app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml
    resources: app/src/main/res/
    build_file: app/build.gradle
    proguard: app/proguard-rules.pro

  build_config:
    gradle_wrapper: 8.2.2
    android_gradle_plugin: 8.2.2
    java_version: 17  # set up via actions/setup-java in CI
    distribution: temurin

  conventions:
    - Prefer Kotlin for new code; legacy Java files may exist in app/src/main/java/
    - Use ViewBinding over findViewById
    - Avoid using deprecated APIs without a fallback
    - All network calls should be async (coroutines or RxJava)
    - Never perform disk I/O or network calls on the main thread

  logging_rules:
    - Log.d() and Log.v() are acceptable in debug builds only
    - Production builds must strip verbose logs via ProGuard/R8
    - Log.d() must NEVER print: GPS coordinates, user tokens, API keys, cartridge solve states
    - Recommended: use BuildConfig.DEBUG guard around verbose logs

  permissions:
    required_by_wherigo:
      - android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION
      - android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION
    optional:
      - android.permission.CAMERA  # for QR cartridge loading
      - android.permission.INTERNET  # for online cartridge fetch
    never_request:
      - READ_CONTACTS
      - READ_CALL_LOG

  testing:
    unit_tests: app/src/test/
    instrumented_tests: app/src/androidTest/
    preferred_frameworks:
      - JUnit4
      - Espresso (UI)
      - Mockito (mocking)

  ci:
    build_command: "./gradlew build"
    lint_command: "./gradlew lint"
    test_command: "./gradlew test"
    publish_command: "./gradlew publish"
wherigo-protocol.yml:
# Wherigo Protocol – Domain Memory
# Agents: read this at the start of every review session.
# Never commit secrets here. This file is static domain knowledge.

project:
  name: Jourwigo
  type: Android native app + React/TypeScript PWA
  purpose: Wherigo cartridge player, designed as a c:geo plugin
  repo: JakeDot/jourwigo-android

wherigo_protocol:
  description: >
    Wherigo is a geocaching game framework by Groundspeak/Lacuna.
    Cartridges are Lua scripts defining zones, tasks, and items.
    The c:geo Android app (open-source geocaching client) supports plugin
    integrations that can launch external apps and exchange data.
  cgeo_plugin_interface:
    manifest_key: metadata.json
    required_permissions:
      - geolocation  # declared in metadata.json requestFramePermissions
    data_exchange: >
      c:geo passes coordinates and cartridge metadata to the plugin.
      The plugin must NOT persist raw GPS coordinates beyond the session.
      All location data should be treated as PII.
  cartridge_format:
    extension: .gwc  # compiled Wherigo cartridge
    engine: OpenWIG / libwherig
  known_gotchas:
    - Log.d() calls that print lat/lon are a privacy risk in production builds.
    - Location accuracy should never be higher than needed for gameplay.
    - Cartridge Lua engine errors should be caught and not crash the host activity.

android_specifics:
  min_sdk: 21  # inferred from project conventions
  target_sdk: 34
  build_tools: AGP 8.2.2 / Gradle 8.2.2
  language: Java (legacy) and Kotlin
  webview_integration: >
    The React PWA frontend may be loaded inside a WebView.
    Ensure WebView settings disable file:// access and javascript injection
    from untrusted sources.

privacy_rules:
  - Never log raw GPS coordinates at Log.d/Log.v level in production code.
  - Location permission must be fine-grained (ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION only when needed).
  - Cartridge progress data should not be sent to external servers without user consent.
